title: Adapt fab_plan_context + fab_get_rule_sections + plan-context to v2.0 dual-root layout
description: Update existing 2 MCP services to scan the new `.fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes}/` + `pending/` directory tree and the dual-root home (`~/.fabric/knowledge/...`). The two-call workflow at plan-context.ts:73 (build description_index → fetch sections via selection_token) is preserved structurally — only data shape and discovery paths change. Adds dual-root scanner: enumerate team root (repo/.fabric/knowledge/) AND personal root (~/.fabric/knowledge/) and merge into a single description_index, tagging each entry with its layer (team/personal) for client filtering. Auto-creates ~/.fabric/knowledge/ on first scan. Does NOT add new MCP tools (those are rc.2/3); does NOT add new section names (existing 4 stay).

files.change: Replace single-root .fabric/rules/ scan with dual-root + multi-subdir scan: (1) repo team root scans `.fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes,pending}/*.md` (note: pending/ included so unreviewed entries are visible to reviewer flows in rc.3, but tagged maturity='proposed'); (2) personal root scans `${os.homedir()}/.fabric/knowledge/{...same subdirs...}/*.md`. Auto-mkdir personal root if missing on first scan. Tag each found node with layer field from frontmatter (or fall back to root inference: repo→team, home→personal). Update toAgentsCompatiblePath at line 559 to handle both roots.

Pass through new knowledge fields (type/maturity/layer) from buildRuleMeta into description_index entries so MCP clients can filter by them. Add filter dimension: exclude maturity='deprecated' from description_index by default (controllable via plan_context input flag, but default is 'do not surface deprecated'). Selection_token TTL state machine (line 70-71) unchanged.

No change to RULE_SECTION_NAMES (4 hard-coded sections stay). Add diagnostic 'missing_knowledge_metadata' (warn-level, not error) when a fetched rule has no type/layer field — flags un-migrated v1.x files. Per Q2 decision in exploration: knowledge types are an orthogonal frontmatter dimension, NOT new section names.

Add optional type/maturity/layer fields to description payload of plan_context output schema. Keep existing fields. Strict-validate that returned entries have either: (a) type+layer set (v2.0) or (b) only summary/intent_clues set (v1.x legacy passthrough during transition). Maturity defaults to 'proposed' if absent.

implementation: Read current findFabricRuleFiles in rule-meta-builder.ts (around line 278 per exploration codebase map)
Refactor to take an array of root paths + array of subdir names
fs.mkdir personal_root recursively with { recursive: true } (idempotent) before scan
Build agentsMetaNode for each; populate layer field (frontmatter wins, else infer from root)
Update plan-context.ts to filter out maturity='deprecated' entries by default
Update rule-sections.ts to emit 'missing_knowledge_metadata' diagnostic for legacy files
Run existing plan-context.test.ts and rule-sections.test.ts — adapt fixtures to new layout where needed
Add new integration test: plan_context with mixed-layer fixtures (1 team + 1 personal) returns merged description_index with correct layer tags

reference.pattern: Existing single-root scan in findFabricRuleFiles; selection_token state machine in plan-context.ts:70
reference.examples: Mirror the existing path-based filtering; add layer dimension as orthogonal axis. Per decision_context line: 'Dual root over single root: gitignore can't filter by frontmatter; personal must be physically separate.' Auto-mkdir on read pattern: see how doctor.ts handles missing .fabric/events.jsonl.

criteria: fab_plan_context returns description_index with team + personal entries merged (verified by integration fixture with 1 entry each)
Each description_index item has `layer: 'team' | 'personal'` field correctly populated from frontmatter
Calling fab_plan_context on first run with no ~/.fabric/knowledge/ creates the directory tree (decisions/, pitfalls/, guidelines/, models/, processes/, pending/) automatically
Files with maturity='deprecated' are excluded from default description_index but discoverable via explicit input flag
fab_get_rule_sections still works on entries from new layout (returns the 4 hard-coded sections when present)
Diagnostic 'missing_knowledge_metadata' fires for an entry without type/layer (warn level, not error — does not break selection)
Existing fab_plan_context tests pass after fixture updates from .fabric/rules/ → .fabric/knowledge/decisions/
Selection_token TTL behavior unchanged (5-min in-memory map still works)
MCP smoke test: spawn server, call fab_plan_context with target=baseline-decision-id, receive token, call fab_get_rule_sections — both succeed

rationale.chosen_approach: Extend existing findFabricRuleFiles to multi-root multi-subdir scan; preserve selection_token semantics
alternatives_considered: Add new MCP tool fab_plan_knowledge_context (rejected — handoff says adapt existing tools, no new MCP in rc.1)
Single-root + frontmatter-layer-only filter (rejected — gitignore cannot filter by frontmatter, personal must be physically separate)
Mount personal as symlink under repo (rejected — leaks personal into git, inverse of intent)
decision_factors: Physical separation of personal/team is a hard requirement
Auto-mkdir reduces friction for first-run users
rationale.tradeoffs: Scanning two roots per request adds I/O; mitigated by existing caching in plan-context (selection_token reuses parsed nodes within TTL)

manual_checks: Run fabric serve, call fab_plan_context via MCP client, inspect description_index payload
Delete ~/.fabric/knowledge/, run again — directory recreated automatically
Add a deprecated entry, verify it's filtered out by default
success_metrics: Mixed-layer fixture produces merged index with correct layer tags 100%
First-run auto-mkdir succeeds on empty home
p95 plan_context latency unchanged vs v1.x baseline (within 10%)
